people.shortProjectTagline: Building interpretable AI systems for alignment research
people.expandedProjectDescription: Working on novel approaches to AI interpretability that make neural networks more transparent and understandable. The research focuses on developing tools that allow researchers to peer inside AI systems and understand their decision-making processes, which is critical for ensuring AI safety and alignment with human values.

people.shortProjectTagline: Developing brain-computer interfaces for cognitive enhancement
people.expandedProjectDescription: Leading research on non-invasive BCIs that can augment human cognitive capabilities. Current work involves developing novel electrode designs and signal processing algorithms that can decode neural activity with unprecedented precision. The long-term goal is to create accessible tools that enhance human cognition and treat neurological conditions.

people.shortProjectTagline: Self-assembling nanomaterials for space infrastructure
people.expandedProjectDescription: Researching programmable matter that can self-assemble in zero-gravity environments. This work combines nanotechnology with space engineering to develop materials that could revolutionize space construction. The project explores how molecular-scale robots could build large structures in orbit without human intervention.

people.shortProjectTagline: AI-driven drug discovery for age-related diseases
people.expandedProjectDescription: Combining machine learning with molecular biology to accelerate the discovery of longevity therapeutics. The research uses AI models to predict which compounds will extend healthy lifespan in model organisms, then validates the most promising candidates in the lab. This work aims to compress decades of traditional drug discovery into months.

people.shortProjectTagline: Democratizing access to neurofeedback technology
people.expandedProjectDescription: Building low-cost, open-source neurofeedback devices that can help people optimize their mental health and cognitive performance. Won the Foresight Existential Hope Prize for work on making advanced neurotechnology accessible to underserved communities worldwide. The project combines hardware design, software development, and community education.

people.shortProjectTagline: Nanorobots for targeted cellular repair
people.expandedProjectDescription: Designing molecular machines that can navigate the human body and repair cellular damage at the nanoscale. This work sits at the intersection of nanotechnology and longevity research, aiming to develop interventions that could reverse aging at the cellular level. Current focus is on prototype nanodevices for mitochondrial repair.

people.shortProjectTagline: Developing closed-loop life support for Mars colonies
people.expandedProjectDescription: Working on regenerative life support systems that could sustain human settlements on Mars. The research focuses on creating self-sustaining ecosystems that recycle air, water, and nutrients with minimal external inputs. This work is critical for making long-term space habitation viable and represents a concrete step toward humanity becoming a multi-planetary species.

people.shortProjectTagline: Neural alignment techniques for safe AI systems
people.expandedProjectDescription: Researching how insights from neuroscience can inform AI safety strategies. The work explores parallels between human value learning and AI alignment, developing techniques that could help AI systems learn and maintain human values more robustly. This interdisciplinary approach combines cognitive neuroscience, machine learning, and ethics.

people.shortProjectTagline: Senolytic therapies using targeted nanoparticles
people.expandedProjectDescription: Developing next-generation senolytic drugs that use nanoparticle delivery systems to specifically target and eliminate senescent cells. These zombie cells accumulate with age and contribute to many age-related diseases. The research aims to create safer, more effective senolytics that could significantly extend healthy lifespan.

people.shortProjectTagline: Cooperative AI frameworks for global coordination
people.expandedProjectDescription: Building AI systems designed for cooperation and coordination at scale. The research addresses how AI can help humanity solve global coordination problems, from climate change to pandemic response. This work combines game theory, multi-agent systems, and mechanism design to create AI that helps humans cooperate more effectively.

people.expandedProjectDescription: Co-founded TruMe Inc - developed TruAge epigenetic test. Built virus detection tech at Xnsion LLC. Now at Genentech doing informatics.
